message.content:
Find and read existing test files in the API that test assembled/integration-level behavior. I need to understand test patterns used in this project.

1. Search for any existing tests in `api/internal/router/`, `api/cmd/server/`, `api/internal/middleware/`
2. Look at test files in `api/internal/auth/` and `api/internal/config/` to understand testing conventions
3. Check for any test helpers in `api/internal/testdb/` or similar
4. Look for httptest usage patterns in the codebase
5. Check if there are any existing router-level or integration-level tests

For each test file found, note:
- Test naming conventions
- How they set up test fixtures (especially for router/handler tests)
- How they handle database dependencies (mocking vs skipping)
- Use of httptest.NewServer or httptest.NewRecorder
- How auth is configured in tests

Read the full content of all relevant test files.

content.text: I'll systematically search for test files and examine test patterns used in this project.

input.description: Find all test files in the api directory

content.text: Now let me read the key test files to understand testing patterns, focusing on integration/router level tests:

content.content:
1â†’package middleware_test
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"crypto/rand"
     5â†’	"crypto/rsa"
     6â†’	"crypto/x509"
     7â†’	"encoding/pem"
     8â†’	"net/http"
     9â†’	"net/http/httptest"
    10â†’	"testing"
    11â†’	"time"
    12â†’
    13â†’	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    14â†’	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5"
    15â†’
    16â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/auth"
    17â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/middleware"
    18â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/testdb"
    19â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/user"
    20â†’)
    21â†’
    22â†’// generateTestRSAKeyPair creates a fresh RSA key pair for testing.
    23â†’func generateTestRSAKeyPair(t *testing.T) (*rsa.PrivateKey, string) {
    24â†’	t.Helper()
    25â†’	key, err := rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, 2048)
    26â†’	if err != nil {
    27â†’		t.Fatalf("generate RSA key: %v", err)
    28â†’	}
    29â†’	pubBytes, err := x509.MarshalPKIXPublicKey(&key.PublicKey)
    30â†’	if err != nil {
    31â†’		t.Fatalf("marshal RSA public key: %v", err)
    32â†’	}
    33â†’	pemBlock := pem.EncodeToMemory(&pem.Block{Type: "PUBLIC KEY", Bytes: pubBytes})
    34â†’	return key, string(pemBlock)
    35â†’}
    36â†’
    37â†’// signRSAToken signs claims with the given RSA private key.
    38â†’func signRSAToken(t *testing.T, key *rsa.PrivateKey, claims auth.Claims) string {
    39â†’	t.Helper()
    40â†’	token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodRS256, &claims)
    41â†’	s, err := token.SignedString(key)
    42â†’	if err != nil {
    43â†’		t.Fatalf("sign RSA token: %v", err)
    44â†’	}
    45â†’	return s
    46â†’}
    47â†’
    48â†’// signHMACToken signs claims with the given HMAC secret.
    49â†’func signHMACToken(t *testing.T, secret string, claims auth.Claims) string {
    50â†’	t.Helper()
    51â†’	token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, &claims)
    52â†’	s, err := token.SignedString([]byte(secret))
    53â†’	if err != nil {
    54â†’		t.Fatalf("sign HMAC token: %v", err)
    55â†’	}
    56â†’	return s
    57â†’}
    58â†’
    59â†’// supabaseClaims returns a Claims struct matching the Supabase JWT format.
    60â†’func supabaseClaims(sub, email string) auth.Claims {
    61â†’	return auth.Claims{
    62â†’		RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
    63â†’			Subject:   sub,
    64â†’			Issuer:    "https://test-project.supabase.co/auth/v1",
    65â†’			Audience:  jwt.ClaimStrings{"authenticated"},
    66â†’			ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(time.Hour)),
    67â†’			IssuedAt:  jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
    68â†’		},
    69â†’		Email: email,
    70â†’		Role:  "authenticated",
    71â†’		AppMetadata: map[string]any{
    72â†’			"provider":  "google",
    73â†’			"providers": []string{"google"},
    74â†’		},
    75â†’		UserMetadata: map[string]any{
    76â†’			"full_name":  "Test User",
    77â†’			"avatar_url": "https://example.com/avatar.png",
    78â†’		},
    79â†’	}
    80â†’}
    81â†’
    82â†’// buildTestRouter creates a chi router with Auth + EnsureUser middleware
    83â†’// and a simple 200 OK handler.
    84â†’func buildTestRouter(validator auth.Validator, userSvc *user.Service) http.Handler {
    85â†’	r := chi.NewRouter()
    86â†’	r.Group(func(r chi.Router) {
    87â†’		r.Use(middleware.Auth(validator))
    88â†’		r.Use(middleware.EnsureUser(userSvc))
    89â†’		r.Post("/graphql", func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {
    90â†’			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
    91â†’			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{"data":{"echo":{"message":"ok"}}}`))
    92â†’		})
    93â†’	})
    94â†’	return r
    95â†’}
    96â†’
    97â†’func TestIntegrationAuth_SupabaseJWTUserCreation(t *testing.T) {
    98â†’	pool := testdb.New(t)
    99â†’	userSvc := user.NewService(pool)
   100â†’
   101â†’	key, pemKey := generateTestRSAKeyPair(t)
   102â†’	validator, err := auth.NewRSAValidator(pemKey)
   103â†’	if err != nil {
   104â†’		t.Fatalf("NewRSAValidator: %v", err)
   105â†’	}
   106â†’
   107â†’	handler := buildTestRouter(validator, userSvc)
   108â†’
   109â†’	// Craft Supabase-format JWT
   110â†’	claims := supabaseClaims("supabase-user-001", "alice@example.com")
   111â†’	tokenStr := signRSAToken(t, key, claims)
   112â†’
   113â†’	// Send authenticated request
   114â†’	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/graphql", nil)
   115â†’	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+tokenStr)
   116â†’	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
   117â†’	handler.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
   118â†’
   119â†’	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
   120â†’		t.Fatalf("expected 200, got %d: %s", rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
   121â†’	}
   122â†’
   123â†’	// Verify user was created in the database
   124â†’	var role string
   125â†’	err = pool.QueryRow(t.Context(),
   126â†’		`SELECT role FROM subscription.team_member WHERE user_id = $1`,
   127â†’		"supabase-user-001",
   128â†’	).Scan(&role)
   129â†’	if err != nil {
   130â†’		t.Fatalf("expected team_member row: %v", err)
   131â†’	}
   132â†’	if role != "OWNER" {
   133â†’		t.Errorf("expected role OWNER, got %s", role)
   134â†’	}
   135â†’
   136â†’	// Verify team was created with email as name
   137â†’	var teamName string
   138â†’	err = pool.QueryRow(t.Context(),
   139â†’		`SELECT t.name FROM subscription.team t
   140â†’		 JOIN subscription.team_member tm ON tm.team_id = t.id
   141â†’		 WHERE tm.user_id = $1`, "supabase-user-001",
   142â†’	).Scan(&teamName)
   143â†’	if err != nil {
   144â†’		t.Fatalf("expected team row: %v", err)
   145â†’	}
   146â†’	if teamName != "alice@example.com" {
   147â†’		t.Errorf("expected team name alice@example.com, got %s", teamName)
   148â†’	}
   149â†’}
   150â†’
   151â†’func TestIntegrationAuth_SupabaseJWT_Idempotent(t *testing.T) {
   152â†’	pool := testdb.New(t)
   153â†’	userSvc := user.NewService(pool)
   154â†’
   155â†’	key, pemKey := generateTestRSAKeyPair(t)
   156â†’	validator, err := auth.NewRSAValidator(pemKey)
   157â†’	if err != nil {
   158â†’		t.Fatalf("NewRSAValidator: %v", err)
   159â†’	}
   160â†’
   161â†’	handler := buildTestRouter(validator, userSvc)
   162â†’
   163â†’	claims := supabaseClaims("supabase-user-002", "bob@example.com")
   164â†’	tokenStr := signRSAToken(t, key, claims)
   165â†’
   166â†’	// Send the same request twice
   167â†’	for i := 0; i < 2; i++ {
   168â†’		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/graphql", nil)
   169â†’		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+tokenStr)
   170â†’		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
   171â†’		handler.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
   172â†’
   173â†’		if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
   174â†’			t.Fatalf("request %d: expected 200, got %d: %s", i+1, rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
   175â†’		}
   176â†’	}
   177â†’
   178â†’	// Verify only one team_member row
   179â†’	var count int
   180â†’	err = pool.QueryRow(t.Context(),
   181â†’		`SELECT count(*) FROM subscription.team_member WHERE user_id = $1`,
   182â†’		"supabase-user-002",
   183â†’	).Scan(&count)
   184â†’	if err != nil {
   185â†’		t.Fatalf("count query failed: %v", err)
   186â†’	}
   187â†’	if count != 1 {
   188â†’		t.Errorf("expected 1 team_member row, got %d", count)
   189â†’	}
   190â†’}
   191â†’
   192â†’func TestIntegrationAuth_SupabaseJWT_HMAC(t *testing.T) {
   193â†’	pool := testdb.New(t)
   194â†’	userSvc := user.NewService(pool)
   195â†’
   196â†’	secret := "supabase-jwt-secret-at-least-32-chars-long!" //nolint:gosec // G101: test credential
   197â†’	validator, err := auth.NewHMACValidator(secret)
   198â†’	if err != nil {
   199â†’		t.Fatalf("NewHMACValidator: %v", err)
   200â†’	}
   201â†’
   202â†’	handler := buildTestRouter(validator, userSvc)
   203â†’
   204â†’	claims := supabaseClaims("supabase-user-003", "carol@example.com")
   205â†’	tokenStr := signHMACToken(t, secret, claims)
   206â†’
   207â†’	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/graphql", nil)
   208â†’	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+tokenStr)
   209â†’	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
   210â†’	handler.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
   211â†’
   212â†’	if rec.Code != http.StatusOK {
   213â†’		t.Fatalf("expected 200, got %d: %s", rec.Code, rec.Body.String())
   214â†’	}
   215â†’
   216â†’	// Verify user was created
   217â†’	var role string
   218â†’	err = pool.QueryRow(t.Context(),
   219â†’		`SELECT role FROM subscription.team_member WHERE user_id = $1`,
   220â†’		"supabase-user-003",
   221â†’	).Scan(&role)
   222â†’	if err != nil {
   223â†’		t.Fatalf("expected team_member row: %v", err)
   224â†’	}
   225â†’	if role != "OWNER" {
   226â†’		t.Errorf("expected role OWNER, got %s", role)
   227â†’	}
   228â†’}
   229â†’
   230â†’func TestIntegrationAuth_MissingHeader(t *testing.T) {
   231â†’	pool := testdb.New(t)
   232â†’	userSvc := user.NewService(pool)
   233â†’
   234â†’	_, pemKey := generateTestRSAKeyPair(t)
   235â†’	validator, err := auth.NewRSAValidator(pemKey)
   236â†’	if err != nil {
   237â†’		t.Fatalf("NewRSAValidator: %v", err)
   238â†’	}
   239â†’
   240â†’	handler := buildTestRouter(validator, userSvc)
   241â†’
   242â†’	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/graphql", nil)
   243â†’	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
   244â†’	handler.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
   245â†’
   246â†’	if rec.Code != http.StatusUnauthorized {
   247â†’		t.Errorf("expected 401, got %d", rec.Code)
   248â†’	}
   249â†’}
   250â†’
   251â†’func TestIntegrationAuth_ExpiredToken(t *testing.T) {
   252â†’	pool := testdb.New(t)
   253â†’	userSvc := user.NewService(pool)
   254â†’
   255â†’	key, pemKey := generateTestRSAKeyPair(t)
   256â†’	validator, err := auth.NewRSAValidator(pemKey)
   257â†’	if err != nil {
   258â†’		t.Fatalf("NewRSAValidator: %v", err)
   259â†’	}
   260â†’
   261â†’	handler := buildTestRouter(validator, userSvc)
   262â†’
   263â†’	claims := auth.Claims{
   264â†’		RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
   265â†’			Subject:   "expired-user",
   266â†’			ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(-time.Hour)),
   267â†’		},
   268â†’		Email: "expired@example.com",
   269â†’	}
   270â†’	tokenStr := signRSAToken(t, key, claims)
   271â†’
   272â†’	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/graphql", nil)
   273â†’	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+tokenStr)
   274â†’	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
   275â†’	handler.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
   276â†’
   277â†’	if rec.Code != http.StatusUnauthorized {
   278â†’		t.Errorf("expected 401, got %d", rec.Code)
   279â†’	}
   280â†’}
   281â†’

1â†’package handler_test
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"encoding/json"
     5â†’	"net/http"
     6â†’	"net/http/httptest"
     7â†’	"net/url"
     8â†’	"testing"
     9â†’
    10â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/handler"
    11â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search"
    12â†’)
    13â†’
    14â†’// â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
    15â†’// ExportCSV â€” unit tests (no real DB)
    16â†’// â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
    17â†’
    18â†’func TestExportCSV_InvalidFiltersJSON(t *testing.T) {
    19â†’	t.Parallel()
    20â†’	reg := search.NewRegistry()
    21â†’	h := handler.ExportCSV(nil, reg, 500)
    22â†’
    23â†’	u := "/api/export/csv?filters=" + url.QueryEscape("not valid json")
    24â†’	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
    25â†’	r := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, u, nil)
    26â†’	h(w, r)
    27â†’
    28â†’	if w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
    29â†’		t.Errorf("HTTP status: got %d, want %d", w.Code, http.StatusBadRequest)
    30â†’	}
    31â†’
    32â†’	var body map[string]string
    33â†’	if err := json.NewDecoder(w.Body).Decode(&body); err != nil {
    34â†’		t.Fatalf("decode body: %v", err)
    35â†’	}
    36â†’	if body["error"] == "" {
    37â†’		t.Error("expected non-empty error message")
    38â†’	}
    39â†’}
    40â†’
    41â†’func TestExportCSV_InvalidFilterField(t *testing.T) {
    42â†’	t.Parallel()
    43â†’	reg := search.NewRegistry()
    44â†’	h := handler.ExportCSV(nil, reg, 500)
    45â†’
    46â†’	filters := `[{"field":"does_not_exist","value":{"value":"x"}}]`
    47â†’	u := "/api/export/csv?filters=" + url.QueryEscape(filters)
    48â†’	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
    49â†’	r := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, u, nil)
    50â†’	h(w, r)
    51â†’
    52â†’	if w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
    53â†’		t.Errorf("HTTP status: got %d, want %d", w.Code, http.StatusBadRequest)
    54â†’	}
    55â†’
    56â†’	var body struct {
    57â†’		Errors []struct {
    58â†’			Field   string `json:"field"`
    59â†’			Message string `json:"message"`
    60â†’		} `json:"errors"`
    61â†’	}
    62â†’	if err := json.NewDecoder(w.Body).Decode(&body); err != nil {
    63â†’		t.Fatalf("decode body: %v", err)
    64â†’	}
    65â†’	if len(body.Errors) == 0 {
    66â†’		t.Error("expected at least 1 validation error")
    67â†’	}
    68â†’}
    69â†’
    70â†’func TestExportCSV_InvalidUserLatLng(t *testing.T) {
    71â†’	t.Parallel()
    72â†’	reg := search.NewRegistry()
    73â†’	h := handler.ExportCSV(nil, reg, 500)
    74â†’
    75â†’	u := "/api/export/csv?userLat=abc&userLng=def"
    76â†’	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
    77â†’	r := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, u, nil)
    78â†’	h(w, r)
    79â†’
    80â†’	if w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
    81â†’		t.Errorf("HTTP status: got %d, want %d", w.Code, http.StatusBadRequest)
    82â†’	}
    83â†’}
    84â†’
    85â†’func TestExportCSV_SortDistanceWithoutLocation(t *testing.T) {
    86â†’	t.Parallel()
    87â†’	reg := search.NewRegistry()
    88â†’	h := handler.ExportCSV(nil, reg, 500)
    89â†’
    90â†’	u := "/api/export/csv?sort=DISTANCE"
    91â†’	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
    92â†’	r := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, u, nil)
    93â†’	h(w, r)
    94â†’
    95â†’	// ValidateRequest should catch sort=DISTANCE without userLocation.
    96â†’	if w.Code != http.StatusBadRequest {
    97â†’		t.Errorf("HTTP status: got %d, want %d", w.Code, http.StatusBadRequest)
    98â†’	}
    99â†’}
   100â†’

1â†’package testdb_test
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"testing"
     6â†’
     7â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/testdb"
     8â†’)
     9â†’
    10â†’// TestNew_ContainerConnectivity spins up the test container (or uses
    11â†’// TEST_DATABASE_URL) and verifies that:
    12â†’//   - a pool connection can be established
    13â†’//   - a basic query executes successfully
    14â†’//   - the schema migrations were applied (search_base.dataset_merged exists)
    15â†’//
    16â†’// Run with: go test -v -run TestNew_ContainerConnectivity ./internal/testdb/
    17â†’func TestNew_ContainerConnectivity(t *testing.T) {
    18â†’	pool := testdb.New(t)
    19â†’	ctx := context.Background()
    20â†’
    21â†’	// 1. Basic connectivity: SELECT 1
    22â†’	var one int
    23â†’	if err := pool.QueryRow(ctx, "SELECT 1").Scan(&one); err != nil {
    24â†’		t.Fatalf("SELECT 1 failed: %v", err)
    25â†’	}
    26â†’	if one != 1 {
    27â†’		t.Errorf("SELECT 1 returned %d, want 1", one)
    28â†’	}
    29â†’
    30â†’	// 2. PostGIS is enabled: ST_AsText on a trivial point
    31â†’	var geomText string
    32â†’	if err := pool.QueryRow(ctx, "SELECT ST_AsText(ST_MakePoint(0,0))").Scan(&geomText); err != nil {
    33â†’		t.Fatalf("PostGIS ST_AsText query failed: %v", err)
    34â†’	}
    35â†’	if geomText == "" {
    36â†’		t.Error("ST_AsText returned empty string")
    37â†’	}
    38â†’	t.Logf("ST_AsText(ST_MakePoint(0,0)) = %s", geomText)
    39â†’
    40â†’	// 3. Write and read against the primary application table.
    41â†’	// institution.state has a FK to location.state, so seed that first.
    42â†’	// Then insert a minimal institution row and count it â€” proves the schema
    43â†’	// is correct, writes are accepted, and reads return the expected value.
    44â†’	_, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
    45â†’		INSERT INTO "location"."state" (code, name, type) VALUES ('MA', 'Massachusetts', 'state')
    46â†’		ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
    47â†’	`)
    48â†’	if err != nil {
    49â†’		t.Fatalf("INSERT into location.state failed: %v", err)
    50â†’	}
    51â†’
    52â†’	_, err = pool.Exec(ctx, `
    53â†’		INSERT INTO "search_base"."institution"
    54â†’			(unit_id, ope6_id, ope8_id, institution_name, city, state)
    55â†’		VALUES
    56â†’			('999999', '999999', '99999900', 'Test University', 'Test City', 'MA')
    57â†’	`)
    58â†’	if err != nil {
    59â†’		t.Fatalf("INSERT into search_base.institution failed: %v", err)
    60â†’	}
    61â†’
    62â†’	var count int
    63â†’	if err := pool.QueryRow(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM "search_base"."institution"`).Scan(&count); err != nil {
    64â†’		t.Fatalf("COUNT query on search_base.institution failed: %v", err)
    65â†’	}
    66â†’	if count == 0 {
    67â†’		t.Error("COUNT(*) from search_base.institution returned 0 â€” expected at least 1 after insert")
    68â†’	}
    69â†’	t.Logf("search_base.institution COUNT(*) = %d", count)
    70â†’}
    71â†’

content.content:
1â†’// Package testdb provides a test helper for acquiring a PostgreSQL database
     2â†’// pool backed by real infrastructure (schema + data). Tests call [New] to
     3â†’// receive a fully-migrated *pgxpool.Pool; the container (when started) and the
     4â†’// pool are both closed automatically via t.Cleanup.
     5â†’//
     6â†’// # Connection priority
     7â†’//
     8â†’//  1. TEST_DATABASE_URL env var â€” connects to a pre-existing database.
     9â†’//  2. A temporary postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine container started via
    10â†’//     testcontainers-go. The container is terminated on test cleanup.
    11â†’//
    12â†’// # Data modes
    13â†’//
    14â†’// Default â€” applies schema migrations and inserts [seedCount] rows of
    15â†’// deterministic fake data (institution + dataset + annual_dataset), then
    16â†’// refreshes all materialized views.
    17â†’//
    18â†’// JSON Fixture â€” when TEST_FIXTURE_DIR is set to a directory containing
    19â†’// JSON fixture files (<schema>.<table>.json or .json.xz), migrations are
    20â†’// applied first, then the JSON data is loaded in FK-safe order via the
    21â†’// fixture package. This is the preferred fixture mode.
    22â†’//
    23â†’// SQL Fixture (legacy) â€” when TEST_FIXTURE_SQL is set to the path of a .sql
    24â†’// or .sql.xz file, that file is decompressed (if needed) and piped through
    25â†’// psql. The fixture must be a complete pg_dump that manages its own schema,
    26â†’// extensions, and data; schema migrations are skipped.
    27â†’package testdb
    28â†’
    29â†’import (
    30â†’	"bufio"
    31â†’	"bytes"
    32â†’	"context"
    33â†’	"database/sql"
    34â†’	"fmt"
    35â†’	"io"
    36â†’	"os"
    37â†’	"os/exec"
    38â†’	"strings"
    39â†’	"testing"
    40â†’	"time"
    41â†’
    42â†’	"github.com/docker/go-connections/nat"
    43â†’	_ "github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/stdlib"
    44â†’	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    45â†’	tc "github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-go"
    46â†’	"github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-go/wait"
    47â†’
    48â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/db"
    49â†’	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/fixture"
    50â†’)
    51â†’
    52â†’const (
    53â†’	postgresImage     = "postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine"
    54â†’	testDBName        = "kapow_test"
    55â†’	testDBUser        = "kapow"
    56â†’	testDBPass        = "kapow"
    57â†’	startupTO         = 90 * time.Second
    58â†’	fixtureEnvVar     = "TEST_FIXTURE_SQL"
    59â†’	fixtureDirEnvVar  = "TEST_FIXTURE_DIR"
    60â†’	seedCount         = 100
    61â†’)
    62â†’
    63â†’// seedStates is the fixed set of states used when generating fake data.
    64â†’// Institutions cycle through these in order, so each state gets seedCount/len(seedStates) rows.
    65â†’// Each entry is {code, name, type} where type matches the location.state.type column.
    66â†’var seedStates = [][3]string{
    67â†’	{"CA", "California", "state"},
    68â†’	{"FL", "Florida", "state"},
    69â†’	{"GA", "Georgia", "state"},
    70â†’	{"IL", "Illinois", "state"},
    71â†’	{"MA", "Massachusetts", "state"},
    72â†’	{"NC", "North Carolina", "state"},
    73â†’	{"NY", "New York", "state"},
    74â†’	{"OH", "Ohio", "state"},
    75â†’	{"PA", "Pennsylvania", "state"},
    76â†’	{"TX", "Texas", "state"},
    77â†’}
    78â†’
    79â†’// New returns a *pgxpool.Pool connected to a test PostgreSQL database. The test
    80â†’// is skipped when testing.Short() is true.
    81â†’//
    82â†’// See package-level documentation for connection priority and data modes.
    83â†’func New(t *testing.T) *pgxpool.Pool {
    84â†’	t.Helper()
    85â†’	if testing.Short() {
    86â†’		t.Skip("testdb: skipped with -short")
    87â†’	}
    88â†’
    89â†’	ctx := context.Background()
    90â†’
    91â†’	dsn := os.Getenv("TEST_DATABASE_URL")
    92â†’	if dsn == "" {
    93â†’		dsn = startContainer(t, ctx)
    94â†’	}
    95â†’
    96â†’	fixturePath := os.Getenv(fixtureEnvVar)
    97â†’	fixtureDir := os.Getenv(fixtureDirEnvVar)
    98â†’
    99â†’	if fixturePath != "" {
   100â†’		// Legacy SQL fixture mode: the SQL dump manages its own schema, extensions,
   101â†’		// and data. Migrations are skipped because the dump recreates everything.
   102â†’		if err := loadFixtureSQL(ctx, t, dsn, fixturePath); err != nil {
   103â†’			t.Fatalf("testdb: load fixture %q: %v", fixturePath, err)
   104â†’		}
   105â†’	} else {
   106â†’		// Both default and JSON fixture modes need migrations.
   107â†’		if err := enablePostGIS(ctx, dsn); err != nil {
   108â†’			t.Fatalf("testdb: enable postgis: %v", err)
   109â†’		}
   110â†’		if err := db.MigrateUp(ctx, dsn); err != nil {
   111â†’			t.Fatalf("testdb: migrate: %v", err)
   112â†’		}
   113â†’	}
   114â†’
   115â†’	pool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, dsn)
   116â†’	if err != nil {
   117â†’		t.Fatalf("testdb: open pool: %v", err)
   118â†’	}
   119â†’	t.Cleanup(pool.Close)
   120â†’
   121â†’	switch {
   122â†’	case fixtureDir != "":
   123â†’		// JSON fixture mode: load from directory of JSON files.
   124â†’		if err := fixture.Load(ctx, pool, os.DirFS(fixtureDir)); err != nil {
   125â†’			t.Fatalf("testdb: load JSON fixtures from %q: %v", fixtureDir, err)
   126â†’		}
   127â†’	case fixturePath == "":
   128â†’		// Default mode: seed fake data.
   129â†’		if err := seedFakeData(ctx, pool); err != nil {
   130â†’			t.Fatalf("testdb: seed fake data: %v", err)
   131â†’		}
   132â†’	}
   133â†’
   134â†’	return pool
   135â†’}
   136â†’
   137â†’// startContainer launches a postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine container, registers
   138â†’// a t.Cleanup to terminate it, and returns the connection DSN.
   139â†’func startContainer(t *testing.T, ctx context.Context) string {
   140â†’	t.Helper()
   141â†’
   142â†’	ctr, err := tc.GenericContainer(ctx, tc.GenericContainerRequest{
   143â†’		ContainerRequest: tc.ContainerRequest{
   144â†’			Image:        postgresImage,
   145â†’			ExposedPorts: []string{"5432/tcp"},
   146â†’			Env: map[string]string{
   147â†’				"POSTGRES_DB":       testDBName,
   148â†’				"POSTGRES_USER":     testDBUser,
   149â†’				"POSTGRES_PASSWORD": testDBPass,
   150â†’			},
   151â†’			WaitingFor: wait.ForLog("database system is ready to accept connections").
   152â†’				WithOccurrence(2).
   153â†’				WithStartupTimeout(startupTO),
   154â†’		},
   155â†’		Started: true,
   156â†’	})
   157â†’	if err != nil {
   158â†’		t.Fatalf("testdb: start container: %v", err)
   159â†’	}
   160â†’	t.Cleanup(func() {
   161â†’		if err := ctr.Terminate(ctx); err != nil {
   162â†’			t.Logf("testdb: terminate container: %v", err)
   163â†’		}
   164â†’	})
   165â†’
   166â†’	host, err := ctr.Host(ctx)
   167â†’	if err != nil {
   168â†’		t.Fatalf("testdb: container host: %v", err)
   169â†’	}
   170â†’	mapped, err := ctr.MappedPort(ctx, nat.Port("5432/tcp"))
   171â†’	if err != nil {
   172â†’		t.Fatalf("testdb: container port: %v", err)
   173â†’	}
   174â†’
   175â†’	return fmt.Sprintf("postgres://%s:%s@%s:%s/%s?sslmode=disable",
   176â†’		testDBUser, testDBPass, host, mapped.Port(), testDBName)
   177â†’}
   178â†’
   179â†’// enablePostGIS runs CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS postgis on the target
   180â†’// database. It is idempotent and safe to call even if PostGIS is already
   181â†’// enabled (e.g. when TEST_DATABASE_URL points to an existing database).
   182â†’func enablePostGIS(ctx context.Context, dsn string) error {
   183â†’	conn, err := sql.Open("pgx", dsn)
   184â†’	if err != nil {
   185â†’		return fmt.Errorf("open: %w", err)
   186â†’	}
   187â†’	defer conn.Close() //nolint:errcheck
   188â†’
   189â†’	if _, err := conn.ExecContext(ctx, "CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS postgis"); err != nil {
   190â†’		return fmt.Errorf("create extension: %w", err)
   191â†’	}
   192â†’	return nil
   193â†’}
   194â†’
   195â†’// seedFakeData inserts [seedCount] deterministic rows into the core tables and
   196â†’// then refreshes all materialized views. The generated rows are stable across
   197â†’// runs (no randomness), so tests can assert on specific values if needed.
   198â†’//
   199â†’// Tables populated:
   200â†’//   - location.state (one row per entry in seedStates)
   201â†’//   - search_base.institution (seedCount rows, cycling through seedStates)
   202â†’//   - search_base.dataset (one row per institution â€” feeds dataset_merged)
   203â†’//   - search_base.annual_dataset (one row per institution â€” feeds dataset_latest)
   204â†’//
   205â†’// All inserts use ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so the function is idempotent.
   206â†’func seedFakeData(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error {
   207â†’	// States â€” prerequisite FK for institution.
   208â†’	for _, s := range seedStates {
   209â†’		if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx,
   210â†’			`INSERT INTO location.state (code, name, type) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`,
   211â†’			s[0], s[1], s[2],
   212â†’		); err != nil {
   213â†’			return fmt.Errorf("seed state %s: %w", s[0], err)
   214â†’		}
   215â†’	}
   216â†’
   217â†’	currentYear := time.Now().Year()
   218â†’
   219â†’	for i := 1; i <= seedCount; i++ {
   220â†’		state := seedStates[(i-1)%len(seedStates)][0]
   221â†’		unitID := fmt.Sprintf("testdb%03d", i)
   222â†’
   223â†’		// Institution row.
   224â†’		if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx,
   225â†’			`INSERT INTO search_base.institution
   226â†’				(unit_id, ope6_id, ope8_id, institution_name, city, state)
   227â†’			 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)
   228â†’			 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`,
   229â†’			unitID,
   230â†’			fmt.Sprintf("t%05d", i), // 6 chars: t + 5 digits
   231â†’			fmt.Sprintf("t%07d", i), // 8 chars: t + 7 digits
   232â†’			fmt.Sprintf("Test University %d", i),
   233â†’			fmt.Sprintf("Test City %d", (i-1)%10+1),
   234â†’			state,
   235â†’		); err != nil {
   236â†’			return fmt.Errorf("seed institution %d: %w", i, err)
   237â†’		}
   238â†’
   239â†’		// Dataset row â€” satisfies the dataset_merged view's combined CTE.
   240â†’		if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx,
   241â†’			`INSERT INTO search_base.dataset
   242â†’				(id, unit_id, theme, datakey, data)
   243â†’			 VALUES ($1, $2, 'CORE', 'test_key', $3)
   244â†’			 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`,
   245â†’			fmt.Sprintf("testdb-ds-%03d", i),
   246â†’			unitID,
   247â†’			fmt.Sprintf(`{"value":%d}`, i),
   248â†’		); err != nil {
   249â†’			return fmt.Errorf("seed dataset %d: %w", i, err)
   250â†’		}
   251â†’
   252â†’		// Annual dataset row â€” satisfies the dataset_latest view.
   253â†’		if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx,
   254â†’			`INSERT INTO search_base.annual_dataset
   255â†’				(id, unit_id, theme, datakey, year, data)
   256â†’			 VALUES ($1, $2, 'CORE', 'annual_test_key', $3, $4)
   257â†’			 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`,
   258â†’			fmt.Sprintf("testdb-ads-%03d", i),
   259â†’			unitID,
   260â†’			currentYear,
   261â†’			fmt.Sprintf(`{"value":%d}`, i),
   262â†’		); err != nil {
   263â†’			return fmt.Errorf("seed annual_dataset %d: %w", i, err)
   264â†’		}
   265â†’	}
   266â†’
   267â†’	// Refresh both materialized views now that the base tables are populated.
   268â†’	for _, view := range []string{
   269â†’		"search_base.dataset_merged",
   270â†’		"search_base.dataset_latest",
   271â†’	} {
   272â†’		if _, err := pool.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW "+view); err != nil {
   273â†’			return fmt.Errorf("refresh %s: %w", view, err)
   274â†’		}
   275â†’	}
   276â†’
   277â†’	return nil
   278â†’}
   279â†’
   280â†’// loadFixtureSQL decompresses (if the path ends with .xz) and pipes the SQL
   281â†’// file through psql against dsn. The SQL is expected to be a complete pg_dump
   282â†’// that manages its own schemas, extensions, data, and materialized view
   283â†’// refreshes.
   284â†’//
   285â†’// psql meta-commands (lines starting with \, other than the COPY terminator \.)
   286â†’// are filtered out before being sent to psql so that non-standard directives
   287â†’// embedded in the dump (e.g. \restrict) do not cause errors.
   288â†’func loadFixtureSQL(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T, dsn, path string) error {
   289â†’	t.Helper()
   290â†’
   291â†’	// Open and optionally decompress the source file.
   292â†’	var rawReader io.Reader
   293â†’	f, err := os.Open(path) //nolint:gosec // test fixture path from trusted source
   294â†’	if err != nil {
   295â†’		return fmt.Errorf("open fixture: %w", err)
   296â†’	}
   297â†’	defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
   298â†’
   299â†’	if strings.HasSuffix(path, ".xz") {
   300â†’		xzCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "xz", "-dc", "--", path) //nolint:gosec // G204: path is from trusted test fixtures
   301â†’		xzOut, err := xzCmd.StdoutPipe()
   302â†’		if err != nil {
   303â†’			return fmt.Errorf("xz pipe: %w", err)
   304â†’		}
   305â†’		var xzStderr bytes.Buffer
   306â†’		xzCmd.Stderr = &xzStderr
   307â†’		if err := xzCmd.Start(); err != nil {
   308â†’			return fmt.Errorf("start xz: %w", err)
   309â†’		}
   310â†’		defer func() { _ = xzCmd.Wait() }()
   311â†’		rawReader = xzOut
   312â†’	} else {
   313â†’		rawReader = f
   314â†’	}
   315â†’
   316â†’	// Filter the SQL stream: drop psql meta-command lines (\ followed by
   317â†’	// anything other than a dot) so that non-standard directives like
   318â†’	// \restrict don't cause psql to error. The COPY terminator \. must be
   319â†’	// preserved.
   320â†’	pr, pw := io.Pipe()
   321â†’	go func() {
   322â†’		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(rawReader)
   323â†’		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 4*1024*1024), 4*1024*1024) // 4 MB per line
   324â†’		for scanner.Scan() {
   325â†’			line := scanner.Text()
   326â†’			if strings.HasPrefix(line, `\`) && line != `\.` {
   327â†’				continue
   328â†’			}
   329â†’			if _, err := fmt.Fprintln(pw, line); err != nil { //nolint:gosec // G705: writing to pipe, not HTTP response
   330â†’				pw.CloseWithError(err) //nolint:errcheck
   331â†’				return
   332â†’			}
   333â†’		}
   334â†’		pw.CloseWithError(scanner.Err()) //nolint:errcheck
   335â†’	}()
   336â†’
   337â†’	// Pipe filtered SQL into psql.
   338â†’	psqlCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "psql", dsn) //nolint:gosec // G204: dsn is from trusted test config
   339â†’	psqlCmd.Stdin = pr
   340â†’	psqlCmd.Stdout = io.Discard
   341â†’	var psqlStderr bytes.Buffer
   342â†’	psqlCmd.Stderr = &psqlStderr
   343â†’
   344â†’	if err := psqlCmd.Run(); err != nil {
   345â†’		t.Logf("testdb: psql stderr:\n%s", psqlStderr.String())
   346â†’		return fmt.Errorf("psql: %w", err)
   347â†’	}
   348â†’	if psqlStderr.Len() > 0 {
   349â†’		t.Logf("testdb: psql warnings:\n%s", psqlStderr.String())
   350â†’	}
   351â†’
   352â†’	return nil
   353â†’}
   354â†’
